name: Hammurabi
subtitle: The king who wrote the law
overview: King of Babylon around 1750 BCE. He carved 282 laws onto a stone pillar and declared them for everyone — kings, priests, and commoners alike. The first legal code of civilization, and the ancestor of every law book you've ever heard of.

name: Cyrus the Great
subtitle: Builder of the first world empire
overview: Founder of the Persian Empire, the largest empire the world had ever seen. Famous for tolerance — he freed captive peoples, respected local religions, and wrote a declaration of human rights two thousand years before anyone else.

subtitle: Pharaoh of Pharaohs

subtitle: The last Pharaoh
